Don't restore fully_validated after MaybeValid externalization

sync_externalized_value_from_ballot was unconditionally restoring
fully_validated=true for validators after externalization, then
maybe_record_externalization re-emitted the cached EXTERNALIZE.
Upstream stellar-core never sets mFullyValidated back to true after
MaybeValid clears it — the local EXTERNALIZE is simply suppressed.

Remove the fully_validated restoration and the forced send_latest_envelope
call, matching stellar-core behavior where MaybeValid externalization
does not re-emit a cached local EXTERNALIZE.

Fixes #1146

commit.message:
fix(bench): remove per-iteration deopt reset that caused 250x regression

The per-iteration ba.reset_maglev_deopt_count() inside b.iter() forced
Maglev to retry on every Criterion iteration even when it consistently
deopts. For deep_object this caused tried=3312 deopted=3312 (all
overflow), adding ~0.5us deopt overhead per iteration (796us total).
Without the reset, Maglev's exponential backoff kicks in after warmup
deopts, letting the interpreter run at full speed (~3us for deep_object).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

removing the main key from IndexMap. Use it in InitProperty's VmFunction

the original insertion order for built-in keys like 'length' and 'name'
when overridden by static class methods.

commit.message:
feat(typeck): bidirectional closure inference infrastructure (#590)

* Initial plan

* feat(typeck): add bidirectional closure inference infrastructure and closure typing tests

Implement the mechanism for pushing expected parameter types into closure
arguments during call expression type checking (bidirectional inference):

- Visit callee before arguments in visit_call_expr so the callee's type
  is available for inference
- For FunctionExpression arguments where the callee expects a Fn type,
  apply expected parameter and return types to unannotated closure params
- Preserve explicitly annotated closure params (never override)

Add unit tests for apply_expected_closure_types (HIR-level) and
integration tests for closure typing scenarios.

Note: End-to-end bidirectional inference through user code requires Fn
type annotation syntax (Fn<P1, ..., Pn, R>), which is planned but not
yet implemented in the parser.

- Rename `expected_param` to `expected_arg_ty` for clarity
- Rename `closure_passed_to_another_closure_ok` test to
  `closure_called_directly_ok` to match actual test behavior

* feat(parser,typeck): implement function type annotations with bidirectional inference

Add support for function type annotation syntax `fn(name: Type, ...) -> RetType`:

- AST: Add `TyKind::Fn(Vec<FunctionTypeParam>, Box<Ty>)` variant and
  `FunctionTypeParam` struct with optional parameter names
- Parser: Parse `fn(...)` in type annotation position, disambiguating
  `name: Type` vs bare `Type` parameters
- AST Lowering: Lower `ast::TyKind::Fn` to `hir::TyKind::Fn`
- AST Visitor: Walk function type params and return type in `walk_ty`
- Analysis: Handle `TyKind::Fn` in `find_node.rs` for IDE support
- Type checker: Add `ty_kinds_compatible()` for structural type comparison
  ignoring `res`/`span` metadata, used in binding/return/argument checks
- Parser snapshot tests for all function type annotation forms
- Type checker tests for bidirectional inference through function types

* feat(grammar): add function type annotation to tree-sitter grammar

  cleaned up but let the function continue and print misleading output

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(TOP/tb): add #1 delays in arbiter3 drive_word to prevent scheduling race

The real root cause was an Xcelium Active-region scheduling race: without
#1, the TB's blocking assignments (src_valid=1, src_data=EOC) happen in
the same Active region as the packet FIFO's always_ff. If the FIFO
evaluates first, it sees old valid=0 and misses the word; on the next
posedge the TB deasserts valid, so the EOC is lost entirely and
packet_count_q never increments → pkt_available stays 0.

Adding #1 after @(posedge clk_sys) moves TB driving to posedge+1ps,
well before the next sample edge — matching the pattern used in
tb_spadmic_stress_arbiter which already passes.

Previous commit's unpacked-array fix (logic src_valid [3]) is kept as
belt-and-suspenders against concurrent bit-select races.

TB issue, NOT an RTL bug.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): extend i32_range to Generic bitwise ops + optimistic Phi seeding

Three codegen analysis improvements:

1. GenericBitwiseOr/And/Xor/Not are unconditionally i32_range producers
   (JS bitwise ops always call ToInt32 per spec). This enables:
   - sum Phi in (sum + i*3) | 0 pattern → i32_range → smi_guarded
   - 32-bit ADD/MUL fast paths instead of 64-bit Smi checks

2. Optimistic Phi seeding in compute_i32_range for loop-counter patterns.
   Phis with at least one i32_range input are optimistically seeded,
   then confirmed by fixed-point (GenericIncrement of i32 Phi → i32).
   This breaks the circular dependency for loop counters like
   for (var i = 0; i < N; i++) where i Phi(0, Inc(i)) is circular.

3. BitwiseOr identity (x | 0) optimization for non-smi-guarded path.
   When right operand is constant 0, skip the right Smi check and OR
   instruction entirely — just Smi-check left and copy.

Expected impact: arithmetic_loop should improve from ~2x to near V8
parity (LEA for *3, skip MOVSXD, fewer Smi checks per iteration).

commit.message:
fix(comparator): use safe three-way pattern for uint64_t qsort comparators (#1816)

* fix(comparator): use safe three-way pattern for uint64_t qsort comparators

Replace `a - b` subtraction (which truncates uint64_t to int, producing
wrong results when the difference exceeds INT_MAX or wraps unsigned) with
the safe `(a > b) - (a < b)` pattern in:

- src/graph/graph_delete_edges.c: _edge_cmp NodeID comparisons
- src/graph/tensor/tensor.c: _value_cmp entity ID comparison
- src/value.c: SIValue_Compare T_NODE/T_EDGE entity ID comparison

Closes #1813

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(comparator): add regression tests for uint64_t overflow in SIValue_Compare

Add unit tests that exercise SIValue_Compare with T_NODE and T_EDGE
entities having large uint64_t IDs (>= 2^32) that trigger the
truncation-to-int overflow bug. Tests verify:

- IDs differing by exactly 2^32 (canonical overflow trigger)
- Antisymmetry: cmp(a,b) and cmp(b,a) have opposite signs
- Equality at large IDs
- IDs differing by 2*2^32
- Near-UINT64_MAX boundary
- Normal small IDs as control

These tests FAIL before the fix (a-b truncation) and PASS after
applying the (a>b)-(a<b) pattern.

Refs #1813
